meta.about_title: About Us — Zhonghai Synergy Group Limited (ZSG)
meta.contact_title: Contact Us — Zhonghai Synergy Group Limited (ZSG)

home.hero_title: Aggregating Global Green Energy, Leading the Carbon-Negative Future of Transportation
home.about_title: Leading the global renewable energy industry
home.about_body: ZSG is achieving the goal of fundamentally reshaping the global energy landscape. We have strategically positioned ourselves at key global shipping hubs—including Sri Lanka, Panama, Egypt, and Indonesia—and taken the lead by investing $11.8 billion in Sri Lanka to develop the first "ZSG Green Hydrogen-Ammonia-Methanol Integrated Project". This initiative marks the world's first integrated base combining the production of green hydrogen, green ammonia, and green methanol. Supported by ancillary projects such as dedicated energy crop production bases and mega-scale wind and solar power farms, we are building a vast "Carbon-Negative Mountain." This is not merely the creation of pioneering, closed-loop renewable energy infrastructure; it is a dedicated contribution toward achieving global "carbon neutrality" and "peak carbon" targets.
home.business_body: We innovatively adopt "Phase Change Migration-Driven Seawater Electrolysis" technology to directly produce high-purity hydrogen, achieving the goal of zero consumption of terrestrial freshwater, eliminating the need for reverse osmosis (RO) facilities, and avoiding concentrated brine discharge. This significantly protects marine animal habitats and coral reef ecosystems. In saline-alkali and drought-prone regions, we establish closed-loop biomass energy crop systems. By cultivating crops such as giant reed and employing the dual technology chain of "Circulating Fluidized Bed Gasification (CGAS)" and "Athermal Carrier Flash Pyrolysis," we maximize carbon emission reductions. ZSG promotes 100% export-oriented, meeting the green fuel bunkering demands of the global shipping market while indirectly boosting the chemical industry influence of the host countries.

about.page_subtitle: Synergizing Transnational Capital and Premier Scientific Matrices
about.section1_body: Zhonghai Synergy Group Limited (ZSG) is headquartered in the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of China, dedicated to the research, development, and large-scale deployment of cutting-edge clean energy technologies, driving the global economy toward a low-carbon and sustainable future. The Group commands a world-leading technology ecosystem spanning green hydrogen, green methanol, and their derivatives, and serves as the initiating party and core operating entity of this project. Backed by substantial transnational capital investment, ZSG has established a strategic presence across major global shipping corridors, with projects spanning Sri Lanka, Panama, Egypt, and Indonesia, building a worldwide clean energy supply network that directly serves the international green marine fuel market.
about.section2_body: The engineering, procurement, and construction mandate is jointly undertaken by China Construction Second Engineering Bureau Third Construction Co., Ltd. and China MCC5 Group Co., Ltd. The former is a subsidiary of China State Construction Engineering Corporation, ranked 14th on the 2024 Fortune Global 500 and the world's largest construction conglomerate, with full-spectrum project management capabilities across the entire asset lifecycle. China MCC5 Group Co., Ltd operates under China Metallurgical Group Corporation and a subsidiary of China Minmetals Corporation, also ranked top 100 in Fortune Global 500, with a long-established global leadership position in industrial engineering and international general contracting. The financing syndicate is led by Zhonglian Construction International Holdings Limited, in coordination with the Export-Import Bank of China, China Export and Credit Insurance Corporation (Sinosure), and etc., establishing a comprehensive project financing and credit approval framework.
about.section3_body: The Group has established deep strategic partnerships with China's foremost research institutions, leading universities, and specialized high-technology enterprises, collectively building a full-chain innovation ecosystem spanning green hydrogen production, methanol synthesis, coal gasification, and air separation technologies. The collaboration network encompasses China CRRC Group Co., Ltd., the Institute of Engineering Thermophysics of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, Tsinghua University, Tianjin University, Shenzhen University, Hainan University, Wuhan Lando Biotechnology Co., Ltd., Tianjin Redsun Science Technology Development Group Co., Ltd., Tianjin Saihong Environmental Engineering Co., Ltd., Hefei Coal Gasification Technology Co., Ltd. under the Chinese Academy of Sciences, and Kaifeng Dier Air Separation Industrial Co., Ltd., providing multi-dimensional support for the Group's core technology research and engineering innovation capabilities.

business.page_subtitle: From core technologies to global projects, building a clean and sustainable green-energy future.
business.area1_body: We use advanced seawater electrolysis to produce high-purity hydrogen directly from seawater. With impurity-ion rejection above 99.99% and zero brine discharge into the ocean, the process protects marine ecosystems and coral reefs. At a DC power consumption as low as 4.5 kWh per standard cubic meter, our hydrogen production sets an industry-leading benchmark for cleaner, more efficient output.
business.area2_body: On marginal and saline-alkali land, we cultivate fast-growing energy crops that yield 75 to 105 tons of dry matter per hectare, then convert them into green methanol and green hydrogen through proven thermochemical processes. This route uses no arable farmland, brings hydrogen production cost to roughly one-third that of conventional electrolysis, and co-produces high-quality biochar to lock away additional carbon.
business.area3_body: By combining biological, chemical, and intelligent mechanical technologies, we bring barren saline-alkali land back to life. Our proprietary soil-conditioning and salt-leaching program can reduce soil salinity from 15‰ to below 4‰, double organic-matter content, and recycle municipal organic waste as a nutrient source. Wasteland could become oasis, laying a solid foundation for cultivating energy crops.
business.area4_body: Our energy crops are exceptional carbon sinks, sequestering over 50 tons of CO₂ per hectare each year, roughly five times that of tropical rainforest. From soil restoration and coal replacement to green-methanol production, each ton of biomass delivers up to 3.04 tons of CO₂-equivalent reduction across its full life cycle, with the entire value chain free of fossil-fuel consumption and benchmarked against international standards such as ISCC Plus and ISO 14001. The projects also create tens of thousands of local jobs and develop local talent in partnership with Chinese Top Academy, helping hosting countries meet their carbon-reduction goals.
business.area5_title: Flagship Project: The Sri Lanka Integrated Production Base
business.area5_body: ZSG is investing USD 11.8 billion in Sri Lanka to build an integrated complex producing green hydrogen, green ammonia, and green methanol. Located along the country's southern and northeastern coasts, the project includes an integrated Green Hydrogen, Green Ammonia, Green Methanol complex reaching over 1.4 million tons of green energy production, a 100,000-hectare energy-crop plantation, a mega-scale wind farm built in phases, and a dedicated chemical-grade export terminal. Construction is scheduled to begin in 2026, with output 100% directed to the global green marine-fuel market.
business.area6_body: Beyond the Sri Lanka flagship, ZSG is advancing several projects in parallel worldwide. ZSG is deploying strategically along the world's major shipping corridors, including the Suez Canal, the Strait of Malacca, Pacific gateway of the Panama Canal, with steady expansion at key nodes such as Egypt and Indonesia to build a worldwide green-fuel supply network.

contact.page_subtitle: Embracing a New Era of Global Green Hydrogen Value Chains. ZSG welcomes long-term concessions, commercial off-take agreements, and strategic partnerships with global maritime lines, chemical buyers, and ESG financing syndicates.
contact.form_success: Thank you for your message. We'll get back to you soon.
